cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to the question: "Why can small pieces of space junk threaten satellites?" The options are A, B, C, or D. First, I'll read through the provided text again to find the relevant information. The passage talks about space junk and how even small pieces can be a threat. The key part is in the second paragraph where it says, "Even bits of space junk as small as dots of paint pose a threat, says Dana. That's because this debris orbits our planet at speeds up to some 28,200 kilometers per hour. Such high speed explains why tiny paint dots have damaged the windshields of space shuttles so badly that they needed to be replaced." So, the reason given here is the high speed of the debris. The text directly states that the high speed is why even small pieces can be damaging. The example given is that paint dots, which are small, have caused damage to space shuttle windshields. Looking at the options: A) They travel at a very high speed. This matches the information in the text. The passage mentions the high speed (28,200 km/h) as the reason for the threat posed by small debris. B) They are difficult to track by satellites. The text doesn't mention anything about tracking difficulty being the main issue. It focuses on the speed causing damage. C) They can blow up into millions of pieces. The passage doesn't state that the small pieces explode. The problem is their speed, not their tendency to explode. D) They are as soft as a ball in the air. This doesn't
